steps.title: Identify what the quotation must show
steps.content: The claim attributes Newland's late arrival to his enjoyment of anticipation — the quotation must reveal that he prefers thinking about a pleasure over actually experiencing it.

steps.title: Find the quotation revealing this
steps.content: Choice B states directly that thinking about a pleasure gave Newland “a subtler satisfaction than its realisation” — explaining the late arrival through his preference for anticipation. The other three address unrelated details: carriage etiquette (A), the fact of late arrival without cause (C), and the opera's beauty (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
